items.topic: AI adoption strategy for the org
items.considerations: Training, tools, governance, risk management
Blameless culture, learning org, psychological safety

AI tools lower cost of direct implementation; staying hands-on builds technical credibility and faster decision loops; recalibrate when to code vs delegate; avoid over-indexing on pure influence at the expense of technical depth; use AI coding assistants to stay current in the codebase

items.topic: AI agent governance and evaluation framework
items.considerations: Eval frameworks for non-deterministic agents; human-in-loop approval gates for high-stakes actions; agent observability (multi-step traces, tool call logs, decision replay); model version governance across agents; safety guardrails and output validators; rollback/kill-switch mechanisms; LLM-as-judge eval at scale

items.topic: Agentic engineering as multi-agent SDLC delivery model
items.considerations: Distinct from AI coding assistants: a control plane that orchestrates cross-team workflows through the full SDLC (plan → code → test → review → deploy) using specialized agents with shared memory and a common observability layer; Cisco/LangChain pilot: 93% reduction in time-to-root-cause debugging, 65% reduction in execution time, biggest gains from compressing testing—not code generation; requires careful role definition (planner, coder, tester, reviewer agents) and traceability across agent handoffs; staff+ engineers become orchestration architects rather than individual contributors; non-determinism and hallucination risk amplify across agent chains

items.topic: Measuring AI-assisted engineering ROI with productivity benchmarks
items.considerations: DX Q1 2026 report (400+ companies): 93% developer AI adoption, AI-authored code at 26.9% of production code (up from 22% Q4 2025); staff+ engineers heaviest agent users at 63.5% regular usage; Cursor users saw 46% PR throughput increase; key nuance: raw code velocity gains are partially offset by new bottlenecks at validation/QA/oversight — measure end-to-end cycle time, not just code generation speed; non-AI bottlenecks (CI/CD latency, code review, deploy gates) still eclipse AI productivity gains for many teams; onboarding time to first productive PR dropped from 39 to 33 days
